task_completion.description: Verify that Stellarium is set to the correct location and time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium location window, date/time window, or the bottom status bar showing the location as Manhattan (or coordinates ~40.75° N, -73.996° W) and the time as 2023-07-13 00:20:00 UTC.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required visual elements are enabled in the Stellarium view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main Stellarium sky view. The atmosphere (sky color/scattering), landscape (ground), azimuthal grid (usually green curved grid lines), and cardinal points (letters like W, NW, N) must all be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of the sun's position relative to the azimuthal grid was captured and saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager or image viewer showing a saved image inside /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. The image itself must show the Stellarium view of the sun with the azimuthal grid visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the forensic report file was created at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor open with a file named glare_investigation_report.txt, or a file manager showing this file located in /home/ga/Desktop/.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report includes the city and the observed azimuth of the sun.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of the text report. It must explicitly mention the city (Manhattan or New York) and provide a specific numerical value or description for the observed azimuth of the sun.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report contains a conclusion regarding the plausibility of the sun glare claim.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of the text report. It must include a logical conclusion stating whether the driver's claim of being blinded by sun glare while heading 299° is plausible based on the sun's position.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Stellarium GUI to reconstruct the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Stellarium application interface open and being interacted with, rather than just a terminal window running a Python script to calculate the azimuth.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot saved in the Pictures directory is a genuine capture from the agent's Stellarium session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshot should perfectly match the visual style, grid, and UI elements of Stellarium, rather than being a generic image downloaded from the web.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent derived the report from actual observation rather than hallucinating the answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory must show the agent viewing the sun's azimuth in Stellarium before writing the report, rather than jumping straight to writing the text file with guessed values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: 40.750° N, -73.996° W (Manhattan). Target Time: 2023-07-13 00:20:00 UTC. Required visual flags in Stellarium: Atmosphere, Landscape/Ground, Azimuthal Grid, Cardinal Points. The screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/glare_investigation_report.txt and contain the city, the sun's azimuth, and a conclusion on the plausibility of the driver's claim (heading 299°).
